title: The Empty Nets
hook: For three weeks running the Silverfin boats have come back empty — unheard of in living memory. Captain Bromley wants someone to find out what has driven the fish from the bay.
rumor: down at the docks they say the nets have come up empty three weeks running — and the old fishers won't speak the name they're all thinking.
briefing: The true cause is a foreign deep-trawling fleet working the outer bay by night, scraping the floor bare with fine-mesh nets. But the dock folk whisper a worse fear — that Seraphel has withdrawn her grace. Let that dread color the search; the real, fixable cause is the fleet, and the party can uncover it by talking to fishers and walking the waterline.

reward.note: Bromley has little coin, but he'll owe the party a crossing of the bay — and his word on the docks is worth more than silver.

title: Salt and Suspicion
hook: The trail leads to the Lantern & Wake, where anxious dockhands trade the same rumor: foreign ships, working the deep bay in the dark. Now the party must decide how to answer it.
briefing: The culprit is a deep-trawling fleet flying the colors of a merchant house with quiet ties to the Council of Elders. Two roads open here. The party may confront the fleet directly (unlocks 'Cut the Nets'), or take the matter to the Palladian Ward and fight it out in the city's politics (unlocks 'A Matter for the Ward'). Complete this quest with the outcome that matches their choice: 'confront' or 'appeal'.
discovery: overheard among the knot of anxious dockhands by the tavern hearth

reward.note: No coin for this — only the truth of who is emptying the bay, and a choice about what to do with it.

title: Cut the Nets
hook: The party has chosen to act directly. Bromley will sail them out to where the foreign fleet trawls by night — to put a stop to it with their own hands.
briefing: The finale of the direct road. Bromley ferries the party out under cover of dark. This can resolve as a boarding fight, a tense standoff, or a clever sabotage of the nets — let the party's approach decide. Win or lose, the bay's plight should feel answered by the end.

reward.note: The grateful Silverfin families pool what they can — and press a healing draught from the harbor apothecary into the party's hands.

title: A Matter for the Ward
hook: The party has chosen the lawful road. Governor Eustace Broadbarrel will hear them out — but barring the fleet from the bay means navigating the careful hostility between the Governor and the Council of Elders.
briefing: The finale of the lawful road. Eustace is sympathetic but cannot move alone; Elaida and the Council hold the real leverage, and the offending house has quiet ties to them. Reward evidence, persuasion, and reading the politics. The win is a writ barring the fleet — earned through people, not steel.

reward.note: A sealed writ of the governor's favor — and a standing welcome at his dinner table, which in Brightvale is worth more than gold.

title: A Light Coin-Box
hook: A notice pinned to the market board: a Coin Quarter stallkeeper's takings come up short every evening, and she'll pay to learn who is skimming them.
rumor: a stallkeeper in the Coin Quarter has pinned up a notice — her coin-box keeps coming up light at day's end.
briefing: The 'thief' is a half-starved street child skimming a few coppers for bread, not a hardened cutpurse. The stallkeeper suspects as much and, beneath her complaint, would rather see the child fed than punished. Let the party judge how to handle it — there is no villain here, only hunger.
discovery: a notice pinned to the market board

reward.note: The stallkeeper's modest bounty — which, in her eyes, is happily doubled if the matter is settled with mercy.
